title: Preserve error provenance when collapsing nested case to with
prompt:
Refactor `MyApp.Signup.create/1` below. When collapsing to `with`, preserve error tags so the caller can distinguish: `{:error, {:invalid_params, reason}}`, `{:error, {:user_exists, _}}`, `{:error, {:db_error, reason}}`. Use pattern-matched normalizers inside the `with` clauses (e.g., `{:ok, _} <- wrap_validate(params)`) so error tagging happens at the boundary and the `with` has no `else` clause at all.

Original:
```
defmodule MyApp.Signup do
  def create(params) do
    case MyApp.Params.validate(params) do
      {:ok, valid} ->
        case MyApp.Users.check_duplicate(valid.email) do
          :ok ->
            case MyApp.Repo.insert_user(valid) do
              {:ok, user} -> {:ok, user}
              {:error, r} -> {:error, {:db_error, r}}
            end

          {:error, r} -> {:error, {:user_exists, r}}
        end
      {:error, r} -> {:error, {:invalid_params, r}}
    end
  end
end
```
tier_rationale: Collapsing the case nest is easy; the trick is preserving the three distinct error tags. Sonnet typically uses a single `else` block (complex-else anti-pattern) instead of normalizing at the clause boundary.
research_citation: Elixir anti-patterns guide: 'Complex else clauses in with'
